chapter_title.en: Chapter on Horses, Keeping Them, and the First to Ride Them

text: اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «كَانَتِ اَلْخَيْلُ وُحُوشاً فِي بِلاَدِ اَلْعَرَبِ وَ صَعِدَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ وَ إِسْمَاعِيلُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَى أَبِي قُبَيْسٍ فَنَادَيَا أَلاَ هَلاَ أَلاَ هَلُمَّ فَمَا بَقِيَ فَرَسٌ إِلاَّ أَعْطَى بِقِيَادِهِ وَ أَمْكَنَ مِنْ نَاصِيَتِهِ ».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2464 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"Horses were once wild in the lands of the Arabs. Ibrahim (as) and Isma‘il (as) ascended Mount Abi Qubays and called out: 'Come! Come forward!'
No horse remained except that it came forward, offering its reins and submitting its forelock."
